[LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copy of the petition or any other paper was received by the assigned examiner, as required LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B., but a cursory review of the petition indicates that the proposed distribution is not pro rata. Is there an agreement? [LRSF 14.35 I.6.a.ii.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/2/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GRACE M. WESTLUND TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Attendance And Testimony Of Rodney R. Westlund At Deposition And For Imposition Of Sanctions Against Respondent And His Attorney Of Record, Thornton Kontz
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the Court is Petitioner Raeanne Grace Westlund's Motion for Order Compelling Attendance and Testimony of Rodney R. Westlund at Deposition and for Imposition of Sanctions Against Respondent and His Attorney of Record, Thornton Kontz, filed August 19, 2024. On November 27, 2024, Respondent filed Declaration of Wendy C. Krog in Response to Petitioner's Motion for Order Compelling Attendance and Testimony of Rodney R. Westlund at Deposition and for Imposition of Sanctions Against Respondent and His Attorney of Record, Thornton Kontz. In it, Krog stated her firm was hired to represent Respondent on October 25, 2024, Rodney Westlund's deposition had been noticed for December 16 and 17, 2024, and the motion to compel was thus premature. On December 2, 2024, the court continued the hearing from December 4, 2024 to January 22, 2025. On December 18, 2024, the parties filed Joint Notice of Motion and Motion to Continue Trial Memorandum of Points and Authorities; Declaration of Wendy C. Krog, hearing date of January 15, 2025. Krog's declaration in support of the joint motion states the parties have agreed to extend the deadline to conduct depositions to March 31, 2025. The papers do not specify whether Rodney Westlund's deposition was taken as noticed, in December. On January 15, 2025, after appearance by counsel at a regularly noticed hearing in Dept 206, the court entered Order on Joint Motion to Continue Trial, ordering in part that all depositions must be completed by March 31, 2025. No further response, opposition, reply, or notice of non-opposition to the motion has been filed. Based on the record before the court, the Motion for Order Compelling Attendance and Testimony of Rodney R. Westlund at Deposition and for Imposition of Sanctions Against Respondent and His Attorney of Record, Thornton Kontz is DENIED AS MOOT. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MOSHE H. ALAFI
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/16/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petition caption is actually incomplete, as it is not merely a report of the status of administration but also a petition requesting additional time to administer the estate. [CRC 7.102.] (2) Please file a copy of the qualified disclaimer reportedly completed on 10/25/23. [Probate Code Sect. 280.] (3) How old are the minors? It appears that a guardian ad litem should now be appointed to represent their interests. (4) It would be helpful if there were some statement or letter from the trustee of the Heskel Trust, attesting to the state of that trust administration to support Executor's request for another five years to administer this estate. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/2/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARITA VILLALTA-MOLINA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing Issues should be addressed in a supplement or an amendment to a petition (or a completely amended petition) that stands on its own, not in response to issues posted in a tentative ruling or to "Examiner's Notes," which just highlights the fact that the petitioner and/or attorney failed to provide sufficient or correct information. No notice is ordinarily required to be given of a supplement to a petition. Notice is required for an amendment. A "declaration in response to probate examiner notes" is neither a supplement nor an amendment. [CRC 7.3, 7.53, and Probate Code Section 1205.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EMILY C. TIMLOW TRUST FBO RICHARD CRAIG HILL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Co-Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/16/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice of Hearing had the incorrect hearing date (year). (2) Additionally, the actual pleading requiring notice was not "Second Amended Petition," etc., as the petitioner did not actually amend the caption of the second "Amended Petition," etc., to correctly distinguish it from the amended petition filed on 9/9/24.. (3) Caron Schmierer was not appointed as the guardian ad litem for the beneficiary until 11/20/24. (Leslie Hill also had no legal authority to sign on behalf of Richard Craig Hill. She did not sign as his attorney-in-fact or conservator. "Mother" is not a fiduciary or representative capacity.) (4) Why is only one remaining co-trustee petitioning? Does the trust permit co-trustees to act separately? (The trust permits a trustee to "resign at any time by giving written notice thereof," but does not appear to provide for the appointment of successor or co-trustees by any trustee.) (5) There was no legal authority for the modification to the trust executed in 2005. Probate Code Section 15403(a) permits beneficiaries of an irrevocable trust to PETITION the court to compel modification (or termination) if all consent. In light of the actions already taken without Court approval, the judicial officer is willing to accept the current guardian ad litem's retroactive consent to the 2005 modification (as well as her consent to the 2024 modification after her appointment as the guardian ad litem). (6) Curiously, the proposed successor co-trustee appears to be the person who was named in the original trust to be the successor trustee of the B1 and B2 subtrusts (now the combined "Hill Trust") after Henry W. Poett, Jr., and Horace Hill III, but the records do not indicate how the third named successor trustee became the acting trustee ahead of the second named successor trustee. Had Stephen Horace Hill declined? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/2/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (It might be in the trust's best interests, going forward, for the trustee(s) to petition for modification of the trust to include clarifying provisions to the original trust.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EDITH C. YATES LIVING TRUST DATED MARCH 14, 2019 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/16/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The ex parte petition was just filed on 1/9/25 but the courtesy copy was not given to the clerk to log in for assignment to an examiner, as required by LRSF 14.11 B. It was received directly by the examiner on 1/14/25. (2) The declaration filed by the attorney (not the trustee petitioner) on 1/9/25 AMENDED the petition. The caption of that pleading is misleading. (A petition is either supplemented or amended; "declarations" are generally meaningless. [See CRC 7.3 for definitions and distinctions.] (3) An amendment requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (4) The only correct form of notice for an 850 petition is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is completed, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was mailed must be attached to the proof of service. (5) After a guardian ad litem is appointed, notice must be given to the guardian ad litem and to all interested parties, including with the notice the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (6) What is the successor trustee's address? (There is no Article Twelve in the trust.) Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/2/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
